speaker: Tim Ferriss
episode_or_content_title: Why you should define your fears instead of your goals (TED2017)
verification_notes:
Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. Tim Ferriss is the author of The 4-Hour Workweek and host of The Tim Ferriss Show (900+ million downloads). His TED2017 talk is an official TED talk available at ted.com with transcript. Tier-1/Tier-2 (widely respected practitioner with major platform). Official URL confirmed: https://www.ted.com/talks/tim_ferriss_why_you_should_define_your_fears_instead_of_your_goals. TED transcripts are not reliably WebFetch-readable; paraphrase is the honest label. The fear-setting concept is thoroughly documented in his tim.blog post at https://tim.blog/2017/05/15/fear-setting/ and in Tribe of Mentors. The specific claims about most-people-choose-unhappiness-over-uncertainty and the asymmetry between feared downside (3/10) and upside (9/10) are documented via web search across multiple secondary sources describing the official TED talk.

[Backfill 2026-06-03] Media fields updated alongside the SIO promotion. Canonical TED slug verified from the existing source_url.
